task_completion.visual_evidence: A data visualization tool (like Panoply) or a script editor is open, showing the 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' file loaded from the PanoplyData directory.

task_completion.description: Create a map visualization of the precipitation rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map plot displaying the 'prate' (precipitation rate) variable is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Configure and export a 12-month looping animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Animation' dialog, time-series configuration, or rendering progress bar is visible, indicating the creation of a video/animation across the 12 months.

task_completion.description: Save the animation file to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, save dialog, or terminal shows 'itcz_animation.mp4' (or similar animation format) saved in the Documents or ITCZ_Lesson directory.

task_completion.description: Write the lesson plan documenting the ITCZ seasonal extremes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open containing the lesson plan. The text MUST explicitly mention that the ITCZ reaches its northernmost extreme in July/August and its southernmost extreme in January/February.

task_completion.description: Save the lesson plan to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, save dialog, or terminal shows 'itcz_lesson_plan.txt' (or 'lesson_plan.txt') saved in the Documents or ITCZ_Lesson directory.

integrity.description: The agent actively generated the animation using the provided data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with Panoply's animation export features or running a visualization script, rather than moving or renaming a pre-existing video file.

integrity.description: The agent examined the data to determine the seasonal extremes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen changing the time/month steps in the visualization tool to observe the ITCZ movement before writing the lesson plan, proving the months were derived from the data and not hallucinated.

integrity.description: The agent composed the lesson plan in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The lesson plan is seen being typed or formatted in a text editor (like gedit, nano, or VS Code), rather than just using a single echo command to blindly create the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' contains the variable 'prate' (precipitation rate). The Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is identified by the band of maximum precipitation. Analysis confirms the ITCZ reaches its northernmost extreme in July/August and its southernmost extreme in January/February. The agent must correctly identify these months in the lesson plan. Acceptable output locations include '/home/ga/Documents/' or '/home/ga/Documents/ITCZ_Lesson/' with filenames 'itcz_animation.mp4' (or similar) and 'itcz_lesson_plan.txt' (or 'lesson_plan.txt').
